note: Each bug is the exact reverse of a real, merged Django fix, the regression that actually shipped before maintainers patched it. BUG_003 reverses a fix for an assigned CVE. No synthetic bugs; every bug links its upstream fix commit.

bugs.severity_note: Recalibrated high->low to match the official NVD rating (low) for CVE-2026-25674 (umask race, overly-permissive permissions in multi-threaded apps). The provenance (a real, assigned CVE) is the point, not an inflated severity.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for CVE-2026-25674. The cache-directory creation goes back to mutating the PROCESS-GLOBAL umask (`os.umask(0o077)`) around `os.makedirs(..., 0o700)` instead of the thread-safe `safe_makedirs` helper. Because umask is process-wide and not thread-safe, a concurrent thread creating files/directories during that window can land with incorrect (too-permissive) permissions, and intermediate directories may not get the intended mode. A real, assigned CVE about incorrect filesystem permissions, reintroduced.

injection.find:
def _createdir(self):
        # Workaround because os.makedirs() doesn't apply the "mode" argument
        # to intermediate-level directories.
        # https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/86533
        safe_makedirs(self._dir, mode=0o700, exist_ok=True)
injection.replace_with:
def _createdir(self):
        # Set the umask because os.makedirs() doesn't apply the "mode" argument
        # to intermediate-level directories.
        old_umask = os.umask(0o077)
        try:
            os.makedirs(self._dir, 0o700, exist_ok=True)
        finally:
            os.umask(old_umask)

bugs.description: Reverts the fix that strips None from an `__in` lookup's right-hand side. Because SQL NULL is never equal to anything, a value of None inside `field__in=[...]` can never match a row, but leaving it in produces a confusing/incorrect IN clause (and historically caused wrong results). The real fix added `rhs.discard(None)` (and the non-hashable fallback `[r for r in self.rhs if r is not None]`). Reverting drops both, so None values leak back into IN clauses.

injection.find:
if self.rhs_is_direct_value():
            # Remove None from the list as NULL is never equal to anything.
            try:
                rhs = OrderedSet(self.rhs)
                rhs.discard(None)
            except TypeError:  # Unhashable items in self.rhs
                rhs = [r for r in self.rhs if r is not None]

bugs.description: Reverts the None guard in `JSONField.bound_data`. When a form with a JSONField is redisplayed with a None value, the code falls straight into `json.loads(data, ...)`; `json.loads(None)` raises `TypeError`, which is NOT caught by the surrounding `except json.JSONDecodeError`, so redisplaying the form crashes. The real fix returned early on None. Reverting reintroduces the crash on None redisplay.

Reverts the fix for a hashed static-file name when the file hash is None/empty. The fixed code uses `('.%s' % file_hash) if file_hash else ''`, falling back to an empty fragment. The reverted code only guards `is not None`, so when `file_hash` is None it stays None and the literal string 'None' is interpolated into the hashed filename (`...rootNoneext`), and an empty string produces a stray '.'. Reintroduces a real broken-filename regression.

injection.replace_with:
if file_hash is not None:
            file_hash = ".%s" % file_hash
